---
title: "March 25: Polling Simulations"
author: 
  - name: Tom Hanna
    id: th
    orcid: 0000-0002-8054-0335
    email: tlhanna@central.uh.edu
    url: tomhanna.me
    affiliation: 
      - name: University of Houston
        city: Houston
        state: TX
        url: uh.edu/class/political-science/
license: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0
copyright: 
  holder: Tom Hanna
  year: 2026
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    style: custom.css
    axe: true
    self-contained: true
    code-fold: false
    logo: ../logo.png
    footer: "POLS3312, Instructor: Tom Hanna"
  revealjs:
    self-contained: true
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    transition: convex
    incremental: true
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    logo: ../logo.png
    footer: "POLS3312, Instructor: Tom Hanna"
  docx:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true
    fig_caption: true
---
      
```{r setup}
#| eval: true
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

set.seed(123)

true_types <- tibble(
  type        = c("A_college_D", "B_noncollege_D",
                  "C_college_R", "D_noncollege_R"),
  pop_share   = c(0.25, 0.25, 0.25, 0.25),
  dem_support = c(0.90, 0.80, 0.10, 0.20)
)

true_dem_share <- sum(true_types$pop_share * true_types$dem_support)

simulate_poll <- function(N = 800, name = "Poll", select_weights) {
  df <- true_types %>%
    mutate(sel_w = select_weights[type],
           prob  = pop_share * sel_w)
  df$prob <- df$prob / sum(df$prob)

  sampled_types <- sample(df$type, size = N, replace = TRUE, prob = df$prob)

  poll <- tibble(type = sampled_types) %>%
    left_join(true_types, by = "type") %>%
    mutate(dem_vote = rbinom(n(), 1, dem_support))

  est <- mean(poll$dem_vote)

  list(est = est)
}

w_poll1 <- c(
  A_college_D    = 1.2,
  B_noncollege_D = 0.7,
  C_college_R    = 1.2,
  D_noncollege_R = 0.7
)

w_poll2 <- c(
  A_college_D    = 0.7,
  B_noncollege_D = 1.2,
  C_college_R    = 0.7,
  D_noncollege_R = 1.2
)

w_poll3 <- w_poll1  # same bias as pollster 1


```

## 2. Function to simulate a single poll


```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-align: "center"
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4

R <- 500  # number of hypothetical repeated polls

sim_P1 <- tibble(
  rep      = 1:R,
  est_P1   = map_dbl(rep, ~ simulate_poll(800, "P1", w_poll1)$est)
)

ggplot(sim_P1, aes(y = est_P1, x = "P1")) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = true_dem_share,
             linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Sampling distribution for one biased pollster (P1)",
    x     = NULL,
    y     = "Estimated Dem two-party share"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

#| echo: false
#| fig-align: "center"
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4

R <- 500  # number of hypothetical repeated polls

sim_P1 <- tibble(
  rep      = 1:R,
  est_P1   = map_dbl(rep, ~ simulate_poll(800, "P1", w_poll1)$est)
)

ggplot(sim_P1, aes(y = est_P1, x = "P1")) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = true_dem_share,
             linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Sampling distribution for one biased pollster (P1)",
    x     = NULL,
    y     = "Estimated Dem two-party share"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)


```

## 3. Three pollsters: opposite vs. shared bias


```{r}
#| eval: true
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-align: "center"



# Pollster 1: over-samples college types, under-samples noncollege
w_poll1 <- c(
  A_college_D    = 1.2,
  B_noncollege_D = 0.7,
  C_college_R    = 1.2,
  D_noncollege_R = 0.7
)

# Pollster 2: mirror image of pollster 1
w_poll2 <- c(
  A_college_D    = 0.7,
  B_noncollege_D = 1.2,
  C_college_R    = 0.7,
  D_noncollege_R = 1.2
)

```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-align: "center"
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4

R <- 500

sim_pairs <- tibble(
  rep       = 1:R,
  est_P1    = map_dbl(rep, ~ simulate_poll(800, "P1", w_poll1)$est),
  est_P2    = map_dbl(rep, ~ simulate_poll(800, "P2", w_poll2)$est),
  est_P3    = map_dbl(rep, ~ simulate_poll(800, "P3", w_poll3)$est)
) %>%
  mutate(
    avg_P1P2 = (est_P1 + est_P2) / 2,  # opposite biases
    avg_P1P3 = (est_P1 + est_P3) / 2   # shared bias
  )

sim_long <- sim_pairs %>%
  select(avg_P1P2, avg_P1P3) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(),
               names_to = "combo",
               values_to = "estimate") %>%
  mutate(combo = recode(combo,
                        avg_P1P2 = "Average P1 & P2\n(opposite bias)",
                        avg_P1P3 = "Average P1 & P3\n(shared bias)"))

ggplot(sim_long, aes(x = combo, y = estimate)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.5, fill = "tan", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = true_dem_share,
             linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Sampling distributions for averages: opposite vs shared bias",
    x     = NULL,
    y     = "Estimated Dem two-party share"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)


```


## 4. Simulate multiple polls per pollster

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-align: "center"

sim_list <- list()

for (k in 1:10) {
  sim_list[[paste0("P1_", k)]] <- simulate_poll(800, paste0("P1_", k), w_poll1)
  sim_list[[paste0("P2_", k)]] <- simulate_poll(800, paste0("P2_", k), w_poll2)
  sim_list[[paste0("P3_", k)]] <- simulate_poll(800, paste0("P3_", k), w_poll3)
}

# Collect estimates
ests <- bind_rows(lapply(names(sim_list), function(nm) {
  tibble(
    poll     = nm,
    pollster = substr(nm, 1, 2),
    est_dem  = sim_list[[nm]]$est
  )
}))

# Composition deviations
comp <- bind_rows(lapply(names(sim_list), function(nm) {
  sim_list[[nm]]$comp %>% mutate(poll_full = nm)
}))

ggplot(ests, aes(x = pollster, y = est_dem)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = true_dem_share,
             linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Poll estimates by pollster",
    y     = "Estimated Dem two-party share",
    x     = "Pollster"
  )

```


## 6. Plot: composition deviations for a few polls

This makes the distributions of voter types explicit.

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-align: "center"


sel_polls <- c("P1_1", "P2_1", "P3_1")

comp_plot <- comp %>%
  filter(poll_full %in% sel_polls) %>%
  mutate(pollster = substr(poll_full, 1, 2))

ggplot(comp_plot, aes(x = type, y = diff_share, fill = pollster)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge()) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +
  labs(
    title = "Sample minus population share by voter type",
    y     = "Sample share - population share",
    x     = "Voter type"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

```
